map.name: F_complex_exp_padded
map.construction: Complex exponential map z=x0+i*x1 -> e^z, padded by idle x2. Conformal (holomorphic), det DF=exp(2*x0)>0, genuinely 2D coupled (rotation-scaling Jacobian). Non-surjective: image misses origin. Dual field X0 solves dz/dt=e^{-z} (w=e^z, dw/dt=1). On orbits with Im(w0)!=0 (full-measure set), w(t)=w0+t never hits 0: X0 COMPLETE. On the null set Im(w0)=0 (branch rays), w(t) hits 0 at finite t: X0 INCOMPLETE, x(t)->-infty. This is a minimal/thin incompleteness example, contrasting A006's open-set incompleteness, and refines the B1 corollary: non-surjectivity forces SOME incompleteness but not necessarily on a positive-measure set.

jacobian.det_constant: exp(2*x0) (not constant in x0, but nonzero and smooth: local diffeo everywhere)

placeholders.star_domain_status: X0 incomplete on null set only; X1 complete (rotation direction, periodic)

literature: classical complex exponential / covering map z->e^z

composition_notes: B001 v0.3 dichotomy example: thin (null-set) vs open-set (A006/A001) incompleteness. Not a Jacobian counterexample; not polynomial.

program_b.b_def: open (null-set incompleteness suggests possibly finite/thin deficiency, not computed)
evidence: w=e^z, dw/dt=1; incomplete iff Im(w0)=0 (measure zero); B1 satisfied minimally
